item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems to carry some emotional weight, but I had to skim.
I can't make it past all the "thauma"…. stuff. If the SCP is a character arc or emotional drama, you should stick to that, and simplify the conprocs and description. When I got into the logs, your characters seemed extremely stiff.
Sorry, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Pertinent" probably isn't the word you were looking for in that interview? I'm not sure the best word to replace it, but usually someone would say "if it's not too personal"

per·ti·nent

adjective
relevant or applicable to a particular matter; apposite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The working name for this was 'a Cure for Loneliness'. The current name came late in the process, so no.

The name is a reference, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. I love this SOOO much. I think that this is so far the best skip in series 5. Excellent job.

By the way, was this inspired by Queen at all? Might just be a coincidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredible and I'm so glad I stumbled onto it. A subtle, "quiet" article with so much depth and feeling, using a great economy of words to draw the reader into it. And I think those may be the best-written interview logs I've read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is really good. something for the foundation to stop and think about intensely before immediately attempting to contain it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one really tugged at my heartstrings. Your character-building and dialogue showcase your strong writing talent, and the premise of the article makes me feel… strange, but in a good way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of SCP-3017 in a very, very good way. I always love character works that go above and beyond the gimmicks and make the character feel real in the manner executed here.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember the reddit thread you posted it in and it still blows me away.

I did notice a small error in the last collapsible:
"Was that why did the Council initiated the dampers?"
The "did" is extraneous I think.

This is beautiful and depressing, wholesome and terrifying, with great characters and just enough information to give a bigger picture.
A fantastic entry for Series V.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Writing Marks in the second interview was hard. I had established him as a sympathetic character in the first interview, but wanted to properly build him up in the second.

I was trying to get a mix of his anger at Gifts' screw up (which he personally authorised), his anger at the sheer misfortune of the disappearance, his frustration that Command has mostly covered the explanation up and thus denied him a degree of closure, his sheer grief over the disappearance of the most important person in the world to him1, his guilt over feeling responsible for the disappearance (since he's the corresponding case of 4042-1), and the resulting mutual sympathy and empathy between Marks and Gifts over their shared experiences.

Took a few repeats, but managed to more-or-less get it all in there.

I'm very glad you appreciated it.

Footnotes
1. I still haven't decided if their relationship was platonic or romantic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was…wow. That's quite a journey. I wonder how Marks actually feels. It must be a feeling too bizarre for me to describe, and hardly could even imagine, yet you captured it perfectly. I'm glad I decided to read this to the end.

I love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has been quite a long journey. It all started with two simple thoughts ("well, I got rejected again" and "what if I wasn't real?") and snowballed into something big.

Here's hoping this goes better this time around.

With deepest thanks to rubberchickenzilla, Zyn, Duban, GabrielJade, DrDromeus, and redditor MrQrtz.
